simpleExplanation.title: Internal and external communication — explained simply
simpleExplanation.summary: Think of internal and external communication as one building block in your Paper 1 (Short Answer and Structured Questions) toolkit. Examiners rarely ask for memorised paragraphs — they want you to apply the idea in a structured answer.
simpleExplanation.analogy: If the full lesson feels dense, focus on one past-paper question: read the mark scheme after attempting it — that shows what Cambridge actually rewards.

faq.q: Is this Business Studies Internal and external communication lesson free?
faq.a: Yes — every topic in our 7115 course is free. MarkScheme offers free past-paper marking so you can turn notes into marks.
Scroll to the past-paper section on this page — we surface real Cambridge questions tagged to 2.4 from our mark-scheme library.

sections.content: Welcome to your **free Business Studies** lesson on **Internal and external communication** (2.4). This is syllabus point 2.4 on **Paper 1 (Short Answer and Structured Questions)** — structured like a premium revision course, with past-paper practice built in.

items: Master the vocabulary examiners use in mark schemes for Internal and external communication
Know which paper (P1) tests this topic most often
Practise at least one official past-paper question after reading
Use “Explain simpler” below if the main lesson feels too advanced

sections.content: Cambridge 7115 rewards answers tied to the case business — name the firm, quote stimulus data, and balance advantages against disadvantages. On Paper 1 (Short Answer and Structured Questions), Internal and external communication often appears as part of a longer structured or case question.
